name: Ice shove

distinctive_features: towering ice formations up to 12 meters high
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: randomly stacked ice blocks forming a ridge or pile

domain_specific_visual.formation_pattern: pushed onto shore by wind and water currents

secondary_functions: creates travel and hunting pathways for Arctic peoples
provides breathing holes for ringed seals

domain_specific_functional.ecological_role: affects sediment dynamics and coastal erosion
domain_specific_functional.climate_interaction: influenced by temperature fluctuations and wind patterns

relationships.description: Ice shoves can cause damage to infrastructure and ecosystems in Arctic regions.
The Iñupiat use ice shoves for travel and hunting.
Ringed seals use ice shoves for maintaining breathing holes.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Arctic regions, Upper Midwest USA, central Canada
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Important for Arctic indigenous peoples for travel and hunting

contrastive_constraints: NOT confined to polar regions only
